subject: Economics
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the multi-dimensional nature of poverty through social indicators
Critically evaluate the methodology of the Poverty Line in India
Understand the intersectionality of social and economic vulnerability
Compare regional and global poverty trends
Assess the impact of historical, economic, and social causes of poverty
Evaluate the effectiveness of targeted anti-poverty programs

lesson_plan.title: Poverty as a Socio-Economic Concept
topics_covered: Beyond income: Social Exclusion and Vulnerability
Case study analysis: Structural vs. Individual poverty
teaching_strategy: Comparative analysis of the Ram Saran and Lakha Singh cases
student_activities: Draft a 'Poverty Profile' for a hypothetical household
Analyze how social exclusion acts as both a cause and a consequence
assessment: Short analytical note on Social Exclusion

lesson_plan.title: Estimating the Poverty Line
topics_covered: The Calorie Norm (2400/2100) and its rationale
Monetary thresholds for Rural vs. Urban areas
teaching_strategy: Lecture on the role of NSSO and World Bank
student_activities: Calculate a monthly budget for a family of 5 at the poverty line
Discuss why the urban poverty line is higher despite lower calorie needs
assessment: Numerical problem on per capita expenditure

lesson_plan.title: Vulnerability and Intersectionality
teaching_strategy: Interpreting bar graphs of poverty ratios by social groups
student_activities: Analyze the 'Vulnerability Graph' from the textbook
Draw a flow-chart showing resource distribution within a poor family

lesson_plan.title: Global and Inter-State Disparities
topics_covered: Success stories: Land reform in WB, HRD in Kerala
Global trends: Decline in China vs. stagnation in Sub-Saharan Africa
teaching_strategy: Analysis of the India Map showing poverty disparities
Lecture on the impact of the Green Revolution in Punjab
student_activities: Plot a graph showing poverty decline in 5 selected states
Compare China’s poverty reduction strategy with India’s

lesson_plan.title: Multi-Factorial Causes of Poverty
topics_covered: Population pressure and low economic growth
The Debt Trap and unequal asset distribution
teaching_strategy: Lecture on the historical roots of Indian poverty
Discussion on the 'Vicious Cycle' of debt
student_activities: Identify 3 historical and 3 modern causes of poverty
Case study analysis of a small farmer's debt cycle

lesson_plan.title: Anti-Poverty Strategy and Programs
topics_covered: Economic growth as a tool for poverty reduction
teaching_strategy: Brainstorming on why schemes fail to reach the target
student_activities: Design an 'Awareness Flyer' for a rural employment scheme
Critically evaluate the Mid-day Meal scheme's impact

lesson_plan.title: The Road Ahead & Final Evaluation
topics_covered: Broadening the goal: From poverty to human dignity

teaching_aids: Bar graphs of vulnerable social groups
teacher_tip: Emphasize that while India has been successful in reducing 'Income Poverty,' the bigger challenge is achieving 'Human Dignity' through education, health, and social equality.
